q:

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
In the given pentapeptide, find out an essential amino acid $(\mathrm{Y})$ and the sequence present in the pentapeptide:

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Among the amino acids present (Serine, Aspartic acid, Glycine, Alanine, and Threonine), only Threonine is classified as an essential amino acid; the others are non-essential or conditionally essential. Therefore, Y = Threonine.

Examining the peptide sequence shown in the structure and cross-referencing with the provided options, the sequence is Thr-Ser-Asp-Gly-Ala from N-terminus to C-terminus.

The answer is option (4): Y is Threonine with sequence Thr-Ser-Asp-Gly-Ala.

question:
Match the LIST-I with LIST-II
List-I
Name of amino acid		List-II
One letter symbol/type
A. ArginineI. D/Non-essential
B. Aspartic acidII. R/Essential
C. LysineIII. E/Non-essential
D. Glutamic acidIV. K/Essential

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Arginine is an essential amino acid and its one-letter symbol is R. Thus, A matches with II.

Aspartic acid is a non-essential amino acid and its one-letter symbol is D. Thus, B matches with I.

Lysine is an essential amino acid and its one-letter symbol is K. Thus, C matches with IV.

Glutamic acid is a non-essential amino acid and its one-letter symbol is E. Thus, D matches with III.

The correct matching is A-II, B-I, C-IV, D-III.

Answer: A-II, B-I, C-IV, D-III

q: The naturally occurring amino acid that contains only one basic functional group in its chemical structure is
question: The naturally occurring amino acid that contains only one basic functional group in its chemical structure is
solution:
Asparagine have only one basic functional Group Structure of Asparagine:

There are three amino acids that have basic side chains at neutral pH. These are arginine (Arg), lysine (Lys), and histidine (His). Their side chains contain nitrogen and resemble ammonia, which is a base. Their pKa's are high enough that they tend to bind protons, gaining a positive charge in the process.

q: From the vitamins <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>12</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi>C</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>D</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>E</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi></math>, the number vitamins that can be stored in our body is ______.
question: From the vitamins <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>6</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mi>B</mi><mn>12</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi>C</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>D</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>E</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>K</mi></math>, the number vitamins that can be stored in our body is ______.
solution:
Fat-soluble vitamins are stored in the body's liver, fatty tissue, and muscles. The  fat-soluble vitamins are vitamins A, D, E, K . These vitamins are absorbed more easily by the body in the presence of dietary fat. Water-soluble vitamins are not stored in the body. The only B vitamin which is folound in liver is B12.

Vitamins A, D, E, K and B12 are stored in liver and adipose tissue.

The number of peptide linkages in it are _____ .
question: A short peptide on complete hydrolysis produces <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> moles of glycine <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi></mfenced></math>, two moles of leucine <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></mfenced></math> and two moles of valine <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi></mfenced></math> per mole of peptide. The number of peptide linkages in it are _____ .
solution:
Peptide linkage is also known as peptide bond. It is an amide formed between −COOH and −NH2 group by elimination of a water molecule. It is represented as
-CO-NH-.

Number of peptide linkages =(Total amino acids -1)

Now, given that short peptide on hydrolysis give seven amino acids.
=7-1=6

q:
Match List - I with List - II.
$\begin{array}{l} \text{List - I} & \text{List - II} \\ \text{Reaction of Glucose with} & \text{Product formed} \\ \text{A. Hydroxylamine} & \text{I. Gluconic acid} \\ \text{B. } \mathrm{Br}_{2} \text{ water} & \text{II. Glucose pentacetate} \\ \text{C. Excess acetic anhydride} & \text{III. Saccharic acid} \\ \text{D. Concentrated } \mathrm{HNO}_{3} & \text{IV. Glucoxime} \end{array}$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
Match List - I with List - II.
$\begin{array}{l} \text{List - I} & \text{List - II} \\ \text{Reaction of Glucose with} & \text{Product formed} \\ \text{A. Hydroxylamine} & \text{I. Gluconic acid} \\ \text{B. } \mathrm{Br}_{2} \text{ water} & \text{II. Glucose pentacetate} \\ \text{C. Excess acetic anhydride} & \text{III. Saccharic acid} \\ \text{D. Concentrated } \mathrm{HNO}_{3} & \text{IV. Glucoxime} \end{array}$
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
Glucose reactions: A + Hydroxylamine forms glucoxime (IV) via condensation with aldehyde group.
B + Br₂ water oxidizes aldehyde to carboxylic acid, forming gluconic acid (I).
C + Excess acetic anhydride acetylates all hydroxyl groups to form glucose pentaacetate (II).
D + Concentrated HNO₃ oxidizes both aldehyde and primary alcohol groups to form saccharic acid (III).
The correct matching is A-IV, B-I, C-II, D-III.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The correct statements are :
A. Activation energy for enzyme catalysed hydrolysis of sucrose is lower than that of acid catalysed hydrolysis.
B. During denaturation, secondary and tertiary structures of a protein are destroyed but primary structure remains intact.
C. Nucleotides are joined together by glycosidic linkage between $\mathrm{C}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{C}_{4}$ carbons of the pentose sugar.
D. Quaternary structure of proteins represents overall folding of the polypeptide chain.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
The correct statements are :
A. Activation energy for enzyme catalysed hydrolysis of sucrose is lower than that of acid catalysed hydrolysis.
B. During denaturation, secondary and tertiary structures of a protein are destroyed but primary structure remains intact.
C. Nucleotides are joined together by glycosidic linkage between $\mathrm{C}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{C}_{4}$ carbons of the pentose sugar.
D. Quaternary structure of proteins represents overall folding of the polypeptide chain.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
Statement A is correct: Enzymes lower the activation energy barrier for reactions, making enzyme-catalyzed hydrolysis of sucrose have lower activation energy than acid-catalyzed hydrolysis.

Statement B is correct: Denaturation disrupts hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions maintaining secondary and tertiary structures, but the covalent peptide bonds forming the primary structure remain intact.

Statement C is incorrect: Nucleotides are joined by phosphodiester linkages between the 3' and 5' carbons of adjacent sugar molecules, not between C1 and C4. Glycosidic linkages connect the nitrogenous base to the C1 carbon of the sugar.

Statement D is incorrect: Quaternary structure describes the arrangement of multiple polypeptide subunits; tertiary structure represents the overall folding of a single polypeptide chain.

Therefore only A and B are correct

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List-I with List-II

  List-I   List-II
A Glucose +HI I Gluconic acid
B Glucose +Br2 water II Glucose pentacetate
C Glucose + acetic anhydride III Saccharic acid
D Glucose +HNO3 IV Hexane

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
question:
Match List-I with List-II

  List-I   List-II
A Glucose +HI I Gluconic acid
B Glucose +Br2 water II Glucose pentacetate
C Glucose + acetic anhydride III Saccharic acid
D Glucose +HNO3 IV Hexane

Choose the correct answer from the options given below
solution:
A) On prolonged heating with HI, Glucose forms n-hexane, suggesting that all the six carbon atoms are linked in a straight chain.

B) Glucose gets oxidised to six carbon carboxylic acid (gluconic acid) on reaction with a mild oxidising agent like bromine water. This indicates that the carbonyl group is present as an aldehydic group.

C) Acetylation of glucose with acetic anhydride gives glucose pentaacetate which confirms the presence of five –OH groups. Since it exists as a stable compound, five –OH groups should be attached to different carbon atoms.

 

D) On oxidation with nitric acid, glucose as well as gluconic acid both yield a dicarboxylic acid, saccharic acid. This indicates the presence of a primary alcoholic (–OH) group in glucose

subject: Chemistry
q: Deficiency of vitamin K causes :
question: Deficiency of vitamin K causes :
solution: Vitamin K refers to structurally similar, fat-soluble vitamins found in foods and marketed as dietary supplements. The human body requires vitamin K for post-synthesis modification of certain proteins that are required for blood coagulation or for controlling binding of calcium in bones and other tissues. Due to deficiency of vitamin K causes increases in blood clotting time.
